doc: Shared UI strings (i18n), en. Same keys as the canonical zh-CN.json — every locale MUST carry the full key set (completeness is conformance-checked). {placeholders} are substituted per platform. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-23-i18n-ui-localization.md.

strings.settings.title: translate-the-damn · Settings
strings.settings.hotkey.hint: Pressing the hotkey translates the current clipboard contents. A failed registration means the hotkey is already in use.
strings.settings.provider.addMessage: Add a generic HTTP backend (OpenAI/Anthropic protocol); after adding, fill in Endpoint/Key/model/protocol, then save.
strings.settings.detect.header: Found {count} importable static API keys on this machine (subscription OAuth excluded). Select the ones to import:
strings.settings.detect.note: Note: imported keys are stored in plaintext in config.json (same as existing keys).
strings.settings.general.configHint: Settings are saved to ~/.translatethedamn/config.json; the API Key stays on this machine only.
strings.settings.target.hint: Unified target language: applies to every backend (injected into CLI/LLM prompts via {target}; google/doubao auto-convert to the matching language code).
strings.settings.auth.httpMissing: ● API Key not set (enter it below)
strings.error.auth: Authentication failed. Sign in or set an API key in Settings.
strings.error.notfound: Command “{command}” not found. Make sure it's installed and on your PATH.
strings.error.badoutput: No translation was returned (a known output issue with this CLI on Windows).
strings.error.apikeyMissing: Set this backend's API Key in Settings.
